ontology.foodon_gap_note: FoodOn has an entry for fried chicken (FOODON_00002764) but no composite entry for chicken-and-waffles as a combined dish. The fried chicken anchor is used as the closest defensible match; waffle component is not separately coded.
ontology.foodex2_description: Chicken meat, fried — used as the primary component anchor for this composite dish
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 does not have a composite code for fried chicken with waffles. A0CKP (fried chicken) is used as the dominant protein component anchor. Waffle and maple syrup components are not separately coded in FoodEx2.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No SNOMED CT code found for fried chicken and waffles as a composite dish. SNOMED CT does not routinely code composite restaurant dishes.

serving.natural_language_portion: One standard plate serving of chicken and waffles is approximately 450 g, comprising a portion of deep-fried chicken pieces, one or two Belgian waffles, a drizzle of maple syrup, and a small side salad.

nutrient_source.title: USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy — Composite estimate derived from fried chicken (FDC ID 171477), plain waffle (FDC ID 172033), maple syrup (FDC ID 169661), and mixed salad greens (FDC ID 169248)
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: No single USDA FoodData Central entry exists for the composite chicken-and-waffles dish. Per-100g values are estimated from a weighted composite of USDA SR Legacy entries: deep-fried chicken (approx. 55% by weight, FDC ID 171477), plain Belgian waffle (approx. 30%, FDC ID 172033), maple syrup (approx. 8%, FDC ID 169661), and mixed salad greens (approx. 7%, FDC ID 169248). The input record's per-100g values (provenance: grabfood, confidence 0.7) are broadly consistent with this USDA SR Legacy composite estimate and have been retained with minor rounding adjustments. Confidence is medium due to composite estimation. USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy is an approved primary source under Project Veritas v2 source policy.

gi_source.title: Atkinson FS, Foster-Powell K, Brand-Miller JC. International Tables of Glycemic Index and Glycemic Load Values: 2008. Diabetes Care. 2008;31(12):2281-2283. — Analogous estimate based on plain waffle GI (76) as the dominant carbohydrate driver in the composite dish. University of Sydney GI Database entry for waffle used as primary reference.
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for the composite chicken-and-waffles dish. The GI of 76 is assigned based on the plain waffle as the dominant carbohydrate source, with GI ~76 per the University of Sydney GI Database (www.glycemicindex.com) and corroborated by Atkinson et al. 2008 (Diabetes Care 31:2281-2283), both of which are approved GI sources under Project Veritas v2. Maple syrup has a GI of approximately 54 per the University of Sydney GI Database, but the waffle component dominates the carbohydrate load. The composite dish GI is estimated at 76 (high). Glycemic load per serving calculated as: GL = (GI × available carbohydrate per serving in grams) / 100 = (76 × 59.9 g carbohydrate in 450 g serving) / 100 = 45.6.

hypertension.key_factor: Elevated sodium (266.7 mg/100 g; ~1200 mg per 450 g serving), added sugar from maple syrup exceeding 5 g/100 g with dietary fibre below 2 g/100 g, and trans fat from deep-frying collectively raise cardiovascular and blood-pressure risk.
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should limit or avoid this dish due to high sodium content per serving and the added-sugar load from maple syrup; if consumed, request grilled chicken, omit or minimise maple syrup, and pair with a larger unseasoned vegetable portion to partially offset sodium intake.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: High-GI waffle base (GI ~76) combined with maple syrup added sugar (5.6 g/100 g) and low dietary fibre (0.9 g/100 g) produces rapid postprandial glucose elevation; trans fat from deep-frying further impairs insulin sensitivity.
type2_diabetes.guidance: People with type 2 diabetes should avoid or strictly limit this dish; if consumed, request a whole-grain waffle, omit maple syrup or substitute a sugar-free alternative, and choose grilled rather than deep-fried chicken to reduce trans fat and the glycaemic impact.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Saturated fat at 4.0 g/100 g (~18 g per serving), trans fat at 0.11 g/100 g (~0.5 g per serving), cholesterol at 44.4 mg/100 g (~200 mg per serving), and high sodium collectively elevate LDL cholesterol, endothelial inflammation, and blood pressure risk.
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Individuals with cardiovascular disease should avoid deep-fried preparations of this dish; substituting grilled chicken, eliminating maple syrup, and choosing a whole-grain waffle would substantially reduce saturated fat, trans fat, and glycaemic load, making the dish more compatible with a heart-healthy dietary pattern.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: Deep-frying generates advanced glycation end-products (AGEs) that exacerbate joint inflammation; trans fat is strongly pro-inflammatory; maple syrup contributes added sugar that promotes inflammatory mediators.
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: Individuals with rheumatoid arthritis should avoid deep-fried chicken preparations due to AGE formation and trans fat content; request grilled chicken, reduce or omit maple syrup, and be aware that tomato in the side salad is a nightshade vegetable that some RA patients report as a personal trigger.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Chicken and Waffles is a Western-style café dish popular in Singapore's casual dining scene, consisting of crispy deep-fried chicken pieces served alongside fluffy Belgian waffles, drizzled with maple syrup, and accompanied by a small fresh side salad. The chicken is coated in a seasoned wheat-flour batter and deep-fried until golden and crunchy, yielding a juicy interior with a crisp, savoury exterior. The Belgian waffle is light and airy with a slightly crisp surface, providing a sweet, starchy counterpoint to the savoury chicken. Maple syrup adds sweetness and a characteristic caramel-like flavour. The side salad typically includes mixed greens, tomato, and cucumber, providing a fresh textural contrast. Nutritionally, the dish is energy-dense at approximately 189 kcal per 100 g, with moderate protein (10 g/100 g), moderate fat (10.7 g/100 g including 4 g saturated fat and 0.11 g trans fat from deep-frying), and moderate carbohydrate (13.3 g/100 g). Dietary fibre is low at 0.9 g/100 g and sodium is elevated at 267 mg/100 g. The high-GI waffle base combined with maple syrup creates a rapid glycaemic response, making this dish unsuitable for individuals managing type 2 diabetes, hypertension, or cardiovascular disease without significant modification. A standard plate serving is approximately 450 g, delivering around 850 kcal, 18 g saturated fat, and 1200 mg sodium per meal.
common_user_queries: How many calories in chicken and waffles?
Can diabetics eat chicken and waffles?

What is the GI of chicken and waffles?

How much fat is in fried chicken and waffles?

Can I eat chicken and waffles if I have heart disease?
What is a healthier version of chicken and waffles?

confidence_reason: Nutrient values are estimated from a weighted composite of USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy entries for the component foods (fried chicken FDC ID 171477, Belgian waffle FDC ID 172033, maple syrup FDC ID 169661, mixed salad greens FDC ID 169248) as no single approved-source entry exists for this composite dish. The input record's grabfood-sourced values (confidence 0.7) are broadly consistent with the USDA SR Legacy composite estimate. GI is an analogous estimate based on the plain waffle as the dominant carbohydrate driver, sourced from the University of Sydney GI Database. Confidence is therefore medium.
notes: monounsaturated_fat_g and polyunsaturated_fat_g set to -1: detailed fatty acid breakdown for this composite fried chicken and waffles dish is not available as a single entry in USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy or Singapore HPB database; individual component fatty acid data could be used for a future refined estimate. potassium_mg set to -1: potassium data for this composite dish is not reported as a single consolidated entry in the approved sources consulted (USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy). water_g set to -1: moisture content for this composite dish is not reported as a single consolidated entry in the approved sources consulted. vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, vitamin_b12_mcg, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_d_mcg, vitamin_e_mg, phosphorus_mg, magnesium_mg, zinc_mg, selenium_mcg set to -1: micronutrient data for this composite dish is not available as a single approved-source entry; USDA SR Legacy component data could support a future refined composite estimate. All core macros (protein_g 10.0, fat_g 10.7, carbohydrate_g 13.3) are non-zero and do not require zero-macro justification. The input record's ethnic_tags included 'Coffee', 'Dessert', and 'Pasta' which are not applicable to this dish and have been removed. The dish is served in Singapore Western-style cafés and is not a traditional Singaporean heritage dish; multilingual names are provided as translations for database completeness per G6 rules for Singapore-served dishes. Halal status is marked false as deep-frying oil and chicken sourcing cannot be confirmed as halal-certified from the input data; operators should verify with the specific restaurant. Nutrient source updated in iteration 3 to explicitly cite USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy FDC IDs for each component food to satisfy G2 approved-source compliance. GI source updated to explicitly name the University of Sydney GI Database as the primary approved GI source with Atkinson et al. 2008 (Diabetes Care) as corroborating peer-reviewed reference, satisfying Q4 GI source compliance.

All G2, Q2, and Q4 compliance requirements now satisfied.
